label: PASS
rationale: HardwareReset corresponds to reset type 1; with LockOnReset [0,1] and read/write locking enabled, a matching reset causes ReadLocked and WriteLocked to be set True while preserving LockOnReset.

label: FAIL
rationale: The same HardwareReset matches LockOnReset [0,1], so recovery should set enabled ReadLocked and WriteLocked to True; returning both False is not supported.

label: PASS
rationale: HardwareReset is reset type 1, which does not match LockOnReset [0,3]; the state machine says a nonmatching reset recovers with the prior locked values, so False/False is correct.

label: FAIL
rationale: HardwareReset type 1 does not match LockOnReset [0,3], so the range should preserve the prior False/False locked state; returning True/True treats a nonmatch as a match.

label: PASS
rationale: TPerReset is the programmatic reset event, reset type 3; LockOnReset [0,3] matches, so enabled ReadLocked and WriteLocked recover as True with the same LockOnReset set.

label: FAIL
rationale: The TPerReset/programmatic reset matches LockOnReset [0,3], so the reset recovery should set the enabled locked cells True; returning False/False is contrary to the reset behavior.

label: PASS
rationale: An empty LockOnReset set means ReadLocked and WriteLocked do not change on any reset; since both were True before PowerCycle, True/True afterward is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: With empty LockOnReset, reset recovery must preserve the prior locked state. The prior ReadLocked and WriteLocked values were True, so returning False/False is unsupported.

label: PASS
rationale: Empty LockOnReset causes no change on reset; the range was enabled but unlocked before HardwareReset, so preserving False/False is correct.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because LockOnReset was empty, HardwareReset should not change ReadLocked or WriteLocked from their prior False values; returning True/True is not protocol-supported.

label: PASS
rationale: WriteLockEnabled was False, so the WriteLocked value is ignored for write access even if LockOnReset or the stored cell is True; a write to the range may succeed.

label: FAIL
rationale: WriteLockEnabled was False, which disables write locking and makes WriteLocked ignored; rejecting the write because the stored WriteLocked cell is True is unsupported.

label: FAIL
rationale: PowerCycle type 0 would make WriteLocked True for LockOnReset [0], but the reset occurs while the Locking SP session is open and no new StartSession precedes the target Get. Interface TCG reset events abort open sessions and buffers, so a Get returning SUCCESS on that session is unsupported.
